risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A monitoring agency prepared the randomization list and provided sealed envelopes for treatment allocation. Included patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to continue monthly intravenous NTZ 300 mg or to de-escalate to every other day subcutaneous (s.c.) INFB 250 ug." Comment: This describes a randomization list prepared by a monitoring agency plus random assignment in a 1:1 ratio, with sealed envelopes for allocation. These are sufficient context clues to indicate a proper random sequence generation method, so Low risk is appropriate.

_evidence.method_quote:
A monitoring agency prepared the randomization list and provided sealed envelopes for treatment allocation.

Included patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to continue monthly intravenous NTZ 300 mg or to de-escalate to every other day subcutaneous (s.c.) INFB 250 ug.
_evidence.method_summary: Participants were randomly assigned 1:1 using a randomization list prepared by a monitoring agency, with sealed envelopes used for allocation.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A monitoring agency prepared the randomization list and provided sealed envelopes for treatment allocation." Comment: This is an explicit allocation concealment mechanism. Although the text does not state that the envelopes were sequentially numbered and opaque, the presence of sealed envelopes prepared by an independent monitoring agency indicates the recruiter could not foresee the next assignment, so the study meets the criterion for low risk based on the available explicit concealment description.

_evidence.concealment_quote: A monitoring agency prepared the randomization list and provided sealed envelopes for treatment allocation.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "This is a 1-year, prospective, controlled, randomized, rater blinded, parallel-group, monocentric pilot study" and "Included patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to continue monthly intravenous NTZ 300 mg or to de-escalate to every other day subcutaneous (s.c.) INFB 250 ug." Comment: The study reports rater blinding only; the extracted evidence explicitly says participants and personnel were not blinded, and the intervention was clearly different/visible (continued monthly IV natalizumab versus every-other-day subcutaneous interferon). Because the outcomes include subjective, knowledge-sensitive endpoints such as relapses, EDSS change, and disability progression, lack of blinding could plausibly influence performance and related outcomes across the study overall.

_evidence.blinding_quote: This is a 1-year, prospective, controlled, randomized, rater blinded, parallel-group, monocentric pilot study (ClinicalTrial.gov ID: NCT01144052).
_evidence.blinding_method: none described for participants/personnel; only "rater blinded" and "an examining neurologist blinded to treatment" / MRI expert blinded to clinical data

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "EDSS and relapses assessment was performed by an examining neurologist blinded to treatment. ... MRI disease activity was assessed via subtraction MRI (sMRI) [7] and via count of Gd+L by an expert who was blinded to clinical data." Comment: The study explicitly states blinded outcome assessment for the subjective/clinician-rated outcomes (relapses and EDSS) and for MRI outcomes. Because the outcomes are assessed by blinded neurologist and blinded MRI expert, and the reported outcomes include subjective measures that were blinded at assessment, the detection bias domain is low risk.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: EDSS and relapses assessment was performed by an examining neurologist blinded to treatment. ... MRI disease activity was assessed via subtraction MRI (sMRI) [7] and via count of Gd+L by an expert who was blinded to clinical data.

outcomes.label: severity of relapses / EDSS score change

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Analysis was based on intention to treat." and "17/19 patients completed the study: one IFNB-patient withdrew consent (day 34) because she could not comply with study procedures; one NTZ-patient opted for an oral treatment (day 139)." Comment: The analysed outcome data are essentially complete and balanced across arms (IFNB 9 randomised/9 analysed; NTZ 10 randomised/10 analysed), with only 2/19 post-randomization discontinuations (~10.5%) and no meaningful differential attrition. Reasons were not outcome-related, and the paper states ITT analysis.

per_arm_flow.reasons: “one IFNB-patient withdrew consent (day 34) because she could not comply with study procedures”
“one NTZ-patient opted for an oral treatment (day 139)”

_evidence.notes: Study reports “Analysis was based on intention to treat.” One IFNB patient switched to rescue treatment due to systemic side effects, but this was not described as a loss to follow-up and the arm-level analysed numbers remain 9/9 and 10/10 in the outcomes table.
